title: Problem 2.3
statement_text: Is the closure of a $\chi$-bounded class under substitution and gluing along a bounded number of vertices also $\chi$-bounded?
context_text: Chudnovsky, Penev, Scott, and Trotignon [3] established that the closure of a $\chi$-bounded class under substitution alone is $\chi$-bounded (with polynomial $\chi$-boundedness preserved), and the same holds for gluing along bounded vertex sets alone. They asked whether combining both operations still preserves $\chi$-boundedness. The problem is also cited in the survey of Scott and Seymour [14].
paper_contribution: Gives a strong negative answer by constructing twincut graphs — a hereditary triangle-free class with unbounded chromatic number that lies in the closure of $\{K_1, K_2\}$ under vertex replication and gluing on stable sets of size at most two, a very special case of the closure considered in the problem.
paper_title: A tamed family of triangle-free graphs with unbounded chromatic number
